column1: # Paren pre-evaluation. Whitespace-separated tokens; `(` and `)` are
# matched and the body is evaluated as a sub-expression. The result
# replaces the paren group on the stack so an outer word's forward
# scan sees concrete values, not paren markers.
# Spec tokenizer: `(` and `)` are bare tokens — surround with spaces.
# Simple inline group, no surrounding word

column3: paren body returns its single result onto the stack

column1: # Group as a forward arg of an outer word

column3: both forward args are paren groups
inner paren evaluates first, outer wraps result
dup leaves two values; both end up on the outer stack
outer add sees the two values produced by the paren
